---
title: "Supplementary material: Event-linked coastal-bird checklist reporting and counts during Pacific herring spawning"
author:
  - name: "Jacob T. Dingwall"
date: ""
format:
  docx:
    reference-doc: ../source_v7/mer_v7_times_new_roman_reference.docx
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
execute:
  enabled: false
lang: en
---

# Scope and governance

This supplement accompanies manuscript v9 and its single conventional nearest-event exposure sensitivity. The work is a post-result exploratory refinement. It does not overwrite the earlier registered analysis or the protected Stage 4A record. Only privacy-safe aggregate results are included; no checklist, observer, locality, source-event identifier, exact coordinate, model object, checkpoint, or record-level derivative is released.

# Estimand and frozen sensitivity design

The primary model retains all concurrent checklist-to-event links additively. The conventional sensitivity assigns each checklist to one minimum-distance event for each checklist with modeled-window links, using a deterministic source-token tie break; checklists without such a link remain in the frame with all exposure terms set to zero. The sensitivity retains the same checklist eligibility, <5 km near and 5–20 km reference zones, six timing periods, A14 active-minus-pre contrast, adjustment variables, three random intercepts, 95% confidence intervals, and 49-species BH families.

The A14 estimate compares the duration-weighted active period (days 0–14) with days −14 to −1 after applying the same baseline-adjusted near/reference construction. Primary estimates are per additional recorded event link; nearest-event estimates use a single selected link. Neither is a percentage change in occupancy or regional abundance.

Release assembly declares the contrast-to-diagnostic relationship as many-to-one across A14/A7 rows and one-to-one after collapse to species–outcome. The primary-diagnostic relationship is many-to-one from the two sensitivity outcomes into the three-outcome primary family. Uniqueness, complete matching, and expected row cardinalities are tested before any release table is written.

## Model formulas and engines

| Analysis | Outcome and engine | Exposure encoding | Model formula |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| nearest_event_sensitivity | Checklist reporting — lme4_glmer_nAGQ0 | for each checklist with modeled-window links, one minimum-distance event retained; checklists without a modeled link retained with all exposure terms zero; deterministic source-token tie break | checklist-reporting indicator [model_response] ~ es_near_baseline + es_reference_baseline + es_near_early_pre + es_reference_early_pre + es_near_immediate_pre + es_reference_immediate_pre + es_near_spawn_start + es_reference_spawn_start + es_near_early_egg + es_reference_early_egg + es_near_late_egg + es_reference_late_egg + factor(checklist_year) + protocol + log_duration + log_effort_distance + observer_count + (1\|event_block_token) + (1\|observer_cluster_token) + (1\|location_cluster_token) |
| nearest_event_sensitivity | Conditional positive numeric reported count — lme4_lmer_REML | for each checklist with modeled-window links, one minimum-distance event retained; checklists without a modeled link retained with all exposure terms zero; deterministic source-token tie break | log(positive numeric reported count) [model_response] ~ es_near_baseline + es_reference_baseline + es_near_early_pre + es_reference_early_pre + es_near_immediate_pre + es_reference_immediate_pre + es_near_spawn_start + es_reference_spawn_start + es_near_early_egg + es_reference_early_egg + es_near_late_egg + es_reference_late_egg + factor(checklist_year) + protocol + log_duration + log_effort_distance + observer_count + (1\|event_block_token) + (1\|observer_cluster_token) + (1\|location_cluster_token) |
| primary_additive_link_and_finite_vs_x | Checklist reporting — lme4_glmer_nAGQ0 | all concurrent event links retained additively | checklist-reporting indicator [model_response] ~ es_near_baseline + es_reference_baseline + es_near_early_pre + es_reference_early_pre + es_near_immediate_pre + es_reference_immediate_pre + es_near_spawn_start + es_reference_spawn_start + es_near_early_egg + es_reference_early_egg + es_near_late_egg + es_reference_late_egg + factor(checklist_year) + protocol + log_duration + log_effort_distance + observer_count + (1\|event_block_token) + (1\|observer_cluster_token) + (1\|location_cluster_token) |
| primary_additive_link_and_finite_vs_x | Conditional positive numeric reported count — lme4_lmer_REML | all concurrent event links retained additively | log(positive numeric reported count) [model_response] ~ es_near_baseline + es_reference_baseline + es_near_early_pre + es_reference_early_pre + es_near_immediate_pre + es_reference_immediate_pre + es_near_spawn_start + es_reference_spawn_start + es_near_early_egg + es_reference_early_egg + es_near_late_egg + es_reference_late_egg + factor(checklist_year) + protocol + log_duration + log_effort_distance + observer_count + (1\|event_block_token) + (1\|observer_cluster_token) + (1\|location_cluster_token) |
| primary_additive_link_and_finite_vs_x | Finite numeric versus X — lme4_glmer_nAGQ0 | all concurrent event links retained additively | finite numeric (1) versus X (0) [model_response] ~ es_near_baseline + es_reference_baseline + es_near_early_pre + es_reference_early_pre + es_near_immediate_pre + es_reference_immediate_pre + es_near_spawn_start + es_reference_spawn_start + es_near_early_egg + es_reference_early_egg + es_near_late_egg + es_reference_late_egg + factor(checklist_year) + protocol + log_duration + log_effort_distance + observer_count + (1\|event_block_token) + (1\|observer_cluster_token) + (1\|location_cluster_token) |

# Comparison summary

| Outcome | Primary estimable | Nearest-event estimable | Sign concordance | Primary BH q < 0.05 | Nearest-event BH positive / negative | Sign reversals | Material interpretation changes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Checklist reporting | 48 | 49 | 34/48 (70.8%) | 13 | 7 positive / 2 negative | 14 | 3 |
| Conditional positive numeric reported count | 46 | 41 | 34/41 (82.9%) | 18 | 19 positive / 0 negative | 7 | 1 |

The nearest-event sensitivity identifies 4 material interpretation change(s) under the fixed pre-result comparison rule. 11/13 primary-BH reporting signs and 18/18 primary-BH count signs were preserved; 7 and 17, respectively, remained BH-significant. The main conclusions therefore require the species- and outcome-specific qualifications listed below; observational and noncausal limitations remain.

# Complete primary and nearest-event A14 results

Table S1 reports every member of the 49-species family for checklist reporting and conditional positive numeric reported count. An em dash marks a failed or unsupported component; the status field is the result for that component. BH-threshold changes alone are not classified as material interpretation changes. Same-direction interval nonoverlap is flagged but is not material because the exposure units differ.

| Species | Outcome | Primary | Nearest event | Same sign | Interpretation classification |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| American Crow | Checklist reporting | 1.02 (0.98–1.08); q=0.509; completed | 1.06 (0.94–1.19); q=0.586; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |
| American Herring Gull | Checklist reporting | 1.39 (1.23–1.57); q=8.51e-07; completed | 1.54 (1.07–2.22); q=0.076; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |
| American Wigeon | Checklist reporting | 1.11 (1.04–1.17); q=0.004; completed | 0.99 (0.87–1.14); q=0.967; completed | no | material_direction_reversal__at_least_one_interval_excludes_zero |
| Bald Eagle | Checklist reporting | 1.04 (0.99–1.09); q=0.203; completed | 1.06 (0.95–1.18); q=0.561; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |
| Barrow's Goldeneye | Checklist reporting | 1.05 (0.97–1.14); q=0.396; completed | 1.07 (0.88–1.30); q=0.773; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |
| Black-bellied Plover | Checklist reporting | 0.94 (0.83–1.07); q=0.527; completed | 0.68 (0.49–0.94); q=0.072; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |
| Black Oystercatcher | Checklist reporting | 0.96 (0.89–1.04); q=0.524; completed | 0.82 (0.66–1.00); q=0.158; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |
| Black Scoter | Checklist reporting | 1.13 (1.00–1.29); q=0.142; completed | 1.85 (1.13–3.06); q=0.063; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |
| Black Turnstone | Checklist reporting | 0.96 (0.84–1.09); q=0.671; completed | 0.89 (0.65–1.21); q=0.706; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |
| Bonaparte's Gull | Checklist reporting | 1.48 (1.22–1.79); q=3.21e-04; completed | 3.89 (2.57–5.90); q=7.48e-09; completed | yes | same_direction__nonoverlapping_intervals__encoding_scales_not_directly_comparable |
| Brandt's Cormorant | Checklist reporting | 0.99 (0.85–1.16); q=0.946; completed | 1.29 (0.88–1.88); q=0.427; completed | no | direction_reversal__both_intervals_include_zero |
| Brant | Checklist reporting | 1.16 (1.04–1.30); q=0.026; completed | 1.37 (1.00–1.89); q=0.158; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |
| Bufflehead | Checklist reporting | 0.94 (0.89–1.00); q=0.110; completed | 0.72 (0.63–0.82); q=1.39e-05; completed | yes | same_direction__nonoverlapping_intervals__encoding_scales_not_directly_comparable |
| California Gull | Checklist reporting | 1.32 (1.21–1.43); q=1.35e-09; completed | 1.57 (1.26–1.96); q=5.07e-04; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |
| Canada Goose | Checklist reporting | 0.99 (0.94–1.05); q=0.857; completed | 1.03 (0.92–1.17); q=0.823; completed | no | direction_reversal__both_intervals_include_zero |
| Common Goldeneye | Checklist reporting | 1.00 (0.93–1.06); q=0.946; completed | 0.83 (0.71–0.96); q=0.063; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |
| Common Loon | Checklist reporting | 0.94 (0.87–1.01); q=0.226; completed | 0.97 (0.80–1.16); q=0.851; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |
| Common Merganser | Checklist reporting | 1.16 (1.10–1.23); q=4.20e-06; completed | 1.39 (1.22–1.60); q=2.22e-05; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |
| Common Murre | Checklist reporting | 0.87 (0.72–1.05); q=0.291; completed | 1.39 (0.85–2.25); q=0.427; completed | no | direction_reversal__both_intervals_include_zero |
| Common Raven | Checklist reporting | 0.94 (0.90–0.99); q=0.051; completed | 0.91 (0.81–1.02); q=0.286; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |
| Double-crested Cormorant | Checklist reporting | 1.03 (0.96–1.10); q=0.571; completed | 1.03 (0.89–1.19); q=0.851; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |
| Dunlin | Checklist reporting | 0.96 (0.86–1.07); q=0.671; completed | 0.89 (0.67–1.16); q=0.608; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |

| Glaucous Gull | Checklist reporting | —; q=—; failed_numerical_fit_no_fallback | —; q=0.997; completed | — | not_assessable__failed_or_unsupported_component |
| Great Blue Heron | Checklist reporting | 1.01 (0.96–1.07); q=0.839; completed | 1.01 (0.89–1.15); q=0.907; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |
| Greater Scaup | Checklist reporting | 1.13 (1.03–1.24); q=0.033; completed | 1.45 (1.13–1.86); q=0.019; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |
| Harlequin Duck | Checklist reporting | 1.15 (1.05–1.26); q=0.013; completed | 0.84 (0.66–1.07); q=0.394; completed | no | material_direction_reversal__at_least_one_interval_excludes_zero |
| Hooded Merganser | Checklist reporting | 1.02 (0.95–1.10); q=0.744; completed | 0.96 (0.81–1.14); q=0.851; completed | no | direction_reversal__both_intervals_include_zero |
| Horned Grebe | Checklist reporting | 0.93 (0.87–1.01); q=0.163; completed | 1.03 (0.86–1.24); q=0.851; completed | no | direction_reversal__both_intervals_include_zero |

| Mallard | Conditional positive numeric reported count | 1.07 (1.04–1.10); q=2.05e-05; completed | 1.18 (1.10–1.26); q=5.42e-06; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |
| Marbled Murrelet | Conditional positive numeric reported count | 1.08 (0.94–1.24); q=0.476; completed | 1.18 (0.87–1.61); q=0.368; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |

| Pacific Loon | Conditional positive numeric reported count | 1.16 (1.04–1.29); q=0.028; completed_with_convergence_warning | 2.12 (1.50–3.00); q=6.37e-05; completed | yes | same_direction__nonoverlapping_intervals__encoding_scales_not_directly_comparable |
| Pelagic Cormorant | Conditional positive numeric reported count | 1.02 (0.98–1.07); q=0.476; completed | 1.05 (0.95–1.17); q=0.398; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |
| Pigeon Guillemot | Conditional positive numeric reported count | 1.02 (0.94–1.10); q=0.772; completed | 1.21 (1.01–1.44); q=0.070; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |
| Red-breasted Merganser | Conditional positive numeric reported count | 1.13 (1.07–1.19); q=8.59e-05; completed | 1.40 (1.23–1.60); q=2.78e-06; completed | yes | same_direction__nonoverlapping_intervals__encoding_scales_not_directly_comparable |
| Red-necked Grebe | Conditional positive numeric reported count | 1.02 (0.96–1.08); q=0.702; completed | 1.22 (1.04–1.44); q=0.029; completed | yes | same_direction__compatible_magnitude |

# Complete component status and diagnostics

Table S2 retains 147 historical primary/finite-number-versus-X components and 98 nearest-event components. Group counts, convergence flags, singularity, rank deficiency, optimizer codes, maximum absolute gradients, all random-intercept variances, residual variance, and failure reasons are displayed. Failed components are not biological null results.

| Analysis | Component | Engine / n / grouping levels | Diagnostics | Variances | Status / failure reason |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| nearest_event_sensitivity | American Crow — Checklist reporting | lme4_glmer_nAGQ0; n=217200; event=58; observer=29248; location=22980 | converged=yes; singular=no; rank deficient=no; optimizer=0; max \|gradient\|=— | event=1.0542; observer=0.8946; location=2.4055; residual=— | completed |

# Interpretation limits

Checklist reporting is not occupancy or formal detection probability. Conditional positive numeric reported count excludes unquantified X reports and conditions on the species being reported with a finite number. The sensitivity does not establish herring consumption, herring-induced movement, or a change in total Strait of Georgia abundance. Habitat, migration, access, event classification, exposure encoding, and observer behaviour remain plausible explanations.

# Machine-readable companion files

- `conventional_exposure_sensitivity_results.csv`: complete 49 × 2 A14 primary-versus-nearest-event comparison.
- `component_status.csv`: complete 245-component status, diagnostics, formulas, and variance release.
- `comparison_summary.csv`: outcome-level estimability, sign concordance, BH, reversal, and material-change counts.
- `interpretation_changes.csv`: components requiring explicit direction, threshold, or interval-compatibility review.
- `sensitivity_execution_record.yml`: authorization, input hashes, code commit, and zero prospective-record access.
